summary: The S550 Mustang GT's 5.0L Coyote V8 is robust and well-proven. The engine is reliable, but the MT-82 manual transmission is a known weakness. The 10-speed auto is the stronger choice.

issues.name: MT-82 Manual Transmission Issues
issues.description: The Getrag MT-82 6-speed manual develops grinding, missed shifts, and difficulty engaging gears. It's the weakest link in the Mustang GT package.

issues.name: Coyote Tick (Injector Noise)
issues.description: The direct injection system on the Coyote V8 creates a ticking noise that sounds concerning but is normal. Louder on cold starts.

issues.name: Oil Consumption (Gen 2 Coyote)
issues.description: 2015–2017 Gen 2 Coyote engines can consume oil at elevated rates. The Gen 3 Coyote (2018+) largely resolved this with improved oil control rings.

issues.name: Rear Axle Whine
issues.description: The factory open differential can develop a whine at highway speeds. More common on 2015–2017 models. Often a fluid change helps.

issues.name: A10 Transmission Shudder
issues.description: The 10-speed automatic can develop a shudder or harsh shift in certain gears. Usually resolved with a transmission software update.

buyingTips: The 10-speed automatic is a better transmission than the MT-82 manual — seriously consider the auto if you want reliability
2018+ (Gen 3 Coyote) is a significant improvement — more power, better oil consumption, and improved transmission options
The Coyote tick is NORMAL — don't let the ticking noise scare you away from an otherwise healthy engine
Performance Pack models get a Torsen LSD, Brembo brakes, and better cooling — strongly preferred over base GT
Check for signs of abuse — burnouts, drag racing, and drifting take their toll on the drivetrain and tires
